evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` exposes a very wide API surface (large prop bag + large return contract) and coordinates drag, zoom, drop, selection, endpoint video, and device behavior in one hook (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:22-140`, `:142-520`).

`useFormSubmission` duplicates orchestration and callback wiring that already exists in `useSubmissionHandlers`/`usePromptQueueHandlers`, creating parallel submission composition paths (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/useFormSubmission.ts:95-138`, `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/useSubmissionHandlers.ts:43-87`, `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/usePromptQueueHandlers.ts:18-37`).

State management boundaries are blurred across UI and async data orchestration: `TasksPane` mixes pane lifecycle, query cache mutation, filtering, pagination, cancellation optimistic updates, and lightbox coordination in one component (`src/shared/components/TasksPane/TasksPane.tsx:42-300`).

Reference-upload hooks combine transport/storage, metadata shaping, optimistic cache mutation, persistence writes, and UI toast/error behavior in single handlers (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/referenceUpload/useStyleReferenceUploadHandler.ts:58-275`, `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/referenceUpload/useResourceSelectHandler.ts:37-203`).

Migration overlap is still present in gallery pagination: the hook advertises a new unified navigation state but still exports backward-compat setters and uses render-time `setTimeout` correction, increasing indirection and cognitive load (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryPagination.ts:92-133`, `:195-226`).

findings.summary: Timeline orchestration hook mixes too many concerns behind a wide bag API
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` takes a broad prop contract and returns a very large command/state object (`useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:22-140`).
Implementation coordinates drag/drop, selection, zoom centering, global events, endpoint drag, and structure-video insertion in one body (`useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:184-520`).
Child timeline UI components (`TimelineItem`, `GuidanceVideoStrip`) carry their own behavior-heavy logic, reinforcing a distributed-but-coupled orchestration model instead of crisp boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Split `useTimelineOrchestrator` into narrower domain hooks with explicit ownership (e.g., `useTimelineViewport`, `useTimelineInteractions`, `useGuidanceVideoPlacement`) and return a smaller composed view-model. Keep media/structure-video commands behind a dedicated adapter module to reduce cross-concern coupling.

findings.summary: Form submission orchestration is duplicated across parallel hook compositions
evidence: `useFormSubmission` manually composes `useSubmissionOrchestrator` + `useSubmitHandler` and wraps queue callbacks (`useFormSubmission.ts:95-138`).

`useSubmissionHandlers` composes the same orchestration pieces and delegates to `usePromptQueueHandlers` for equivalent callback wrappers (`useSubmissionHandlers.ts:43-87`, `usePromptQueueHandlers.ts:18-37`).
This creates two structural ways to assemble the same behavior, increasing drift risk when submission flow changes.
findings.suggestion: Choose a single composition entrypoint (prefer `useSubmissionHandlers` + `usePromptQueueHandlers`) and refactor `useFormSubmission` into a thin delegator or remove one path entirely. Add focused tests for the chosen composition seam so future changes don’t fork behavior.

findings.summary: Reference upload handlers mix IO pipeline, cache writes, and UI concerns in one layer
evidence: `useStyleReferenceUploadHandler` includes file conversion, storage uploads, thumbnail creation, metadata construction, optimistic cache updates, and settings persistence inside one callback (`useStyleReferenceUploadHandler.ts:58-275`).
`useResourceSelectHandler` combines selection business rules with optimistic cache mutation + persistence writes (`useResourceSelectHandler.ts:37-203`).
The hook-level contracts in `referenceManagement/types.ts` become broad because infrastructure and domain policy are not separated.
findings.suggestion: Extract a reference-domain service layer (`createReferencePointer`, `uploadAndProcessReference`, `persistReferenceSelection`) and keep hooks focused on UI event wiring + transient UI state. Let hooks call one application service command per user action.

findings.summary: Tasks pane component still bundles data orchestration and rendering concerns
evidence: `TasksPane` manages filter/session persistence, pagination, status aggregation, optimistic cancellation updates, sliding-pane lifecycle, and lightbox handoff in one component (`TasksPane.tsx:62-300`).
It directly mutates query cache with cancellation logic while also owning pane UI states (`TasksPane.tsx:245-291`).
`PanesContext` already centralizes pane lock/open behavior (`PanesContext.tsx:6-224`), but `TasksPane` still carries significant pane-state choreography, indicating boundary overlap.
findings.suggestion: Move task-data orchestration (filters, counts, cancellation optimistic updates, page scope) into a dedicated controller hook/service and leave `TasksPane` as a presentational shell. Keep pane lifecycle interactions behind a narrow adapter to `PanesContext`.

findings.summary: Gallery pagination carries both new state machine and legacy control API
evidence: `useMediaGalleryPagination` introduces unified `navigationState` but retains backward-compat mutators `setLoadingButton` and `setIsGalleryLoading` (`useMediaGalleryPagination.ts:92-107`, `:195-226`).

The same hook also performs render-time correction via `setTimeout(() => setRawPage(page), 0)` (`useMediaGalleryPagination.ts:121-133`), which is a mechanical workaround rather than a clean state transition boundary.

`useMediaGalleryActions` is another broad behavior bucket (delete/backfill timing, lightbox shaping, download transport, tick UI), amplifying unclear ownership at the gallery hook layer (`useMediaGalleryActions.ts:133-260`).
findings.suggestion: Complete the migration by removing legacy setter APIs, move page correction into explicit effect/event transitions, and split `useMediaGalleryActions` into focused hooks (`useGalleryDeleteFlow`, `useGalleryDownload`, `useGalleryLightboxBridge`) aligned with the unified pagination model.
